title: [main.c:105-109] get_eui64 failure path misses lichen_lora_l2_deinit call
description:
Completeness: Cleanup ordering category.

In init_standalone_lora() at lines 105-109:


The cleanup label at line 125-128:


This is correct - stop() then deinit() is the proper sequence.

BUT: get_eui64() returns NULL only when state == LORA_UNINIT (line 936-939). But at line 105, we've already called init() (line 91) and start() (line 99). So state is LORA_RUNNING, and get_eui64() should never return NULL unless there's a race.

If get_eui64() somehow returned NULL despite being in RUNNING state, that indicates a serious bug. The current error handling is correct but doesn't distinguish 'expected failure' from 'should be impossible'.

Consider adding a more specific error message or assertion for this case.
close_reason: Fixed: added proper cleanup (goto fail_after_start) and improved error message to 'internal state error'
